prerequisites: An Awin advertiser account with Admin access to the account you want to pull data from. Awin requires Admin permissions to create an API token (check Account > Account Permissions).
Your Awin advertiser account ID, used for the connector's required advertiserId config field.
A personal Awin OAuth 2.0 API token, supplied as the connector's api_key secret field.
Decide your backfill window and pagination: start_date (the date to begin syncing from), lookback_days, and step_increment, all required config fields.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 personal token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the api_key secret field, which holds an Awin OAuth 2.0 personal API token. Awin expects it in the request as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'. The token is tied to your personal Awin user account rather than to a single advertiser, so it can access every account your user is permitted to see.

setupSteps.title: Confirm Admin access
setupSteps.body: Before creating a token, make sure your Awin user has Admin access to the advertiser account you want to sync. For advertisers, Awin checks this under Account > Account Permissions.

setupSteps.title: Create your Awin API token
setupSteps.body: Go to https://ui.awin.com/awin-api, or click the 'API Credentials' link in the user menu at the top right of the Awin UI. Enter your login password, then click 'Show my API token'. Your personal OAuth 2.0 token is created and can be copied to your clipboard. Paste it into the connector's api_key field.

setupSteps.title: Provide your advertiser ID
setupSteps.body: Set the required advertiserId config field to your Awin advertiser account ID, which scopes the data the connector pulls.

setupSteps.title: Configure the sync window
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date, lookback_days, and step_increment config fields to control how far back the connector backfills and how it paginates through the date range.
